info.parenttitle: B3.3 Mechanical systems application and selection (HL only)

markdown:
## Levers and Load-Effort Systems


<definition term="Lever">A rigid bar that **pivots on a fulcrum** to **move a load with an applied effort**.</definition>

<callout type="note">#### Recap of Key Terms


* **Load (L):** The object or resistance that needs to be moved.
* **Effort (E):** The input force applied to move the load.
* **Fulcrum:** The fixed pivot point of the lever.
* **Load Arm:** Distance from fulcrum to the load.
* **Effort Arm:** Distance from fulcrum to where effort is applied.</callout>

![1st, 2nd and 3rd class levers (firgelli)](https://cdn.sanity.io/images/wla69xp2/production/ebd68068f1b63c0f00137c5fb951844801825c76-668x330.png)

### Formula


To calculate **effort or load**, use:

$$\text{Effort} × \text{Effort Arm} = \text{Load} × \text{Load Arm}$$

<callout type="note">For a lever to be **balanced**, the **clockwise** **moments** must **equal** the **counterclockwise** **moments**.</callout>

#### **Rearranged versions:**


$$\text{Load} = \frac{\text{Effort} × \text{Effort Arm}}{\text{Load Arm}}$$

$$\text{Effort} = \frac{\text{Load} × \text{Load Arm}}{\text{Effort Arm}}$$

<example_question>
        <question>
        A lever has a load of 50 N located 2 m from the fulcrum. The effort is applied 4 m from the fulcrum. Calculate the effort.
        </question>
        <answer>
        $$\text{Effort}=\frac{50×2}{4}​=25 \text{ N}$$
        </answer>
      </example_question>
